meta.title: Scheduling — pouk.ai
meta.description: cal.pouk.ai is pouk.ai's self-hosted scheduling app. Connect Google Calendar to share real availability and let people book time without the back-and-forth.

hero.title: Book time without the back-and-forth.
hero.lede: cal.pouk.ai is pouk.ai's self-hosted scheduling app. Connect your Google Calendar, share one link, and let people book real availability — no double-bookings, no email tag.

intro: Scheduling sounds simple until you are three emails deep trying to find a slot that still exists. cal.pouk.ai removes that loop: you publish a booking page, it reads your calendar, and people pick a time that is genuinely free.
The app is self-hosted by pouk.ai and built on Cal.com / Cal.diy. The Google Calendar integration is optional — but when you connect it, the app stays in sync with the calendar you already live in.

scopes.heading: How the Google Calendar integration is used
scopes.intro: When you connect Google Calendar, cal.pouk.ai requests the minimum Calendar access needed to schedule on your behalf. Each permission maps to a feature you can see:

items.heading: Reading your availability
items.body: The app reads your existing calendar events and busy/free times so your booking page only ever offers slots that are actually open. Nothing is shown to the people booking with you beyond "free" or "busy."

items.heading: Creating booking events
items.body: When a booking is confirmed, the app writes the event to your Google Calendar — and updates or cancels it if the booking changes — so the meeting lands in the calendar you already check.

items.heading: Keeping in sync
items.body: Because the app reads availability at the moment it is needed, your booking page reflects your real schedule even as it changes throughout the day.

privacyNote.text: We request only Calendar and basic-profile access — never Gmail, Drive, or Contacts — and our use of Google data follows the Google API Services User Data Policy, including Limited Use. Full detail, and how to disconnect or delete your data, is in the
